The process is valid. We can be, we can have different levels of sophistication about it depending on how much we prepare and how much information we gather and use before we get to the sales process. Yeah that's the exact procedure I use when I was selling done for you, doing done for you webinars and tele-seminars and launches for people. They'd have to qualify and then they had to fill out an application but the application really told me what their pain was, what they were trying to achieve, what was holding them back. Then when I got them on the telephone, well I have their hot buttons that they just told me openly what they are, which makes the selling process, makes the selling process that more valuable. I think Dan brought up a good point that we don't want to gloss over because a lot of people won't do this. A lot of sales people won't do the back. They can't do it. They can't do it. They won't do the background information so they won't do any level of research about the clients that they're in front of. There's so much information, Google is easy and especially in the B2B world people are putting their lives out on in the public domain. So really doing that background you allow you to tease out hot buttons before you even get into the equation and allows you to have some level of rapport building skills that you'll have the ability to do when you're in front of that person also. Yeah one of the things that brings up a big thing is a lot of people I think would think well I can't do that. I can't send out the questionnaire ahead of time. I can't and that's wrong thinking first of all because in most cases you probably can. And doesn't that position you better if you want to go as a consultant, right? I mean before people get to you for your consulting day, which I'm going to come to in just a minute because it's important because I don't think people think that you sell actually, you know. But before they come to you they got to jump through all of these hoops to get to you. And for the most part? For the most part right? Every once in a while there's a leaper who appears to be qualified. Right. About 50% of the time I regret that. Right. Because I'm now with a poorly prepared person. Right. And unlike many I don't use a formal questionnaire. Right. People like J. Abraham for example do. Right. But yeah there's a series of qualifying hoops that if they don't do anything else they tell me a who to stop before we go any further by behavior that is you know markedly incompatible with what I'm going to have to have in order to be effective with this person. They also teach the person before they get to the sales event what to expect. So there's a certain amount of expectation management that goes into a diagnostic consultative or sophisticated selling process. Part of this is you have to decide in advance what he's going to have to know and accept in order to buy from you or do business with you. So for me that's extraordinarily high fees and limited access. Those are going to be the two deal breakers that at the end when it's actually time to say to prescribe now what you need is these 18 things and I can do 16 of them for you and here's what that calculates out to and how do you want to take care of the money. The two things that will be deal breakers which will have made everything else a waste of time. Getting to that point. For me the two deal breakers are going to be what do you mean I can't pick up the phone and talk to you. What do you mean I have to wait two weeks for an appointment. What do you mean you don't use email. That's going to be a deal breaker or the amount of money is going to be a deal breaker. Can't afford it or just can't mentally wrap their head around the idea that it can cost this much. Or that you're going to take a piece of the revenue in addition to the fees. So those two things are going to be the deal breakers for me. So I want to get that out of the way. Before you meet them. Before we get to face to face even though I'm paid to do the selling. I because it's a paid consultation day rather than a comp consultation. Still I'm not interested in that money. I'm interested in the big money. So I don't I don't even I don't want 19 grand for the day if there's no reasonable hope right of us going further. So
